title: What to Expect in Markets this Week: Inflation Data, Warsh Testimony in Congress, Bank Earnings
text: Inflation will loom large this week, with the government releasing new Consumer Price Index data and big bank earnings shedding light on how Americans are keeping up.

title: Earnings estimates have been following an unusual pattern this time around
text: Analysts generally lower their estimates in the months before earnings. But thanks to the energy and tech sectors, expectations have actually climbed heading into second-quarter results.

title: The Credit Market Is Sending A Warning On AI Stocks
text: Credit default swap spreads are widening for both hyperscalers and semiconductor giants, signaling rising caution in the credit markets. Oracle, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, Nvidia, AMD, Intel, and Broadcom are experiencing CDS spread increases, with some reaching multi-month highs.

title: Talks Falter as Hormuz Violence Grows
text: Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar and ABC News' Shannon Kingston say renewed attacks in and around the Strait of Hormuz have undermined fragile diplomatic efforts, with Gulf allies pressing to revive negotiations as shipping and regional security remain under threat. They say technical talks continue to face resistance from hardline factions in Iran, while Sen.

title: The Truce Is Loose
text: U.S. equity markets pushed higher ahead of earnings season, brushing off rising benchmark interest rates and a more unfavorable turn in the Middle East conflict as Iran risks resurfaced. Iran war developments returned to center stage as the tentative U.S.-Iran ceasefire unraveled after another round of strikes and Iran's reported closure of the Strait of Hormuz rattled markets again. Tanker-tracking data showed Hormuz flows recovering, but momentum faded into the weekend, lifting oil and gasoline from multi-month lows as traders repriced renewed risks to physical energy flows again.

title: The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
text: As earnings season kicks off, AI investment remains front and center.

title: Frontline: Q2 Is Already In The Bank - Maintaining Strong Buy
text: Frontline plc remains a Strong Buy with a $52 price target, implying 42% upside from current levels. Q2 earnings are largely locked in, with 82% of VLCC days fixed at $181,700/day, supporting a potential 9.3% quarterly dividend yield. Spot and time charter rates remain robust post-peace framework, with structural tightness expected through 2027 despite a record orderbook.

title: Wall Street Week Ahead
text: Second-quarter earnings season shifts into high gear this week, with major banks kicking off results alongside key inflation reports and major reads on AI spending.
